settings.label: The Science
settings.headline: Why Your Skin Develops Uneven Pigmentation
settings.body:
Hyperpigmentation is your skin's response to a trigger. The most common trigger is sun exposure, but it can also be caused by hormonal changes (melasma), inflammation (post-acne marks), or even just the natural ageing process. These triggers send a signal to specialised cells called melanocytes to produce an excess of melanin, the natural pigment that gives skin its colour.

Think of it as a 'pigmentation factory' with a five-stage assembly line. It starts with the initial trigger, which activates the master switch for melanin production, a transcription factor known as MITF. This switch tells the 'enzyme factory' to produce tyrosinase, the key enzyme responsible for melanin synthesis. The newly made melanin is then packaged and transferred to your surface skin cells in a process that can be inhibited by up to 68% with the right ingredients.

Many common treatments only target one stage of this factory, leading to frustratingly slow or temporary results. For years, the standard has been hydroquinone — now banned for cosmetic use in Australia, the EU, and Japan due to safety concerns.

settings.label: Our Approach
settings.headline: A Clinical, Multi-Pathway Approach to Pigmentation
settings.body: At Aliangé, we don't just treat the symptom; we target the entire pigmentation pathway. Dr. Alison Jamieson, with her 40 years of clinical expertise, understands that lasting clarity cannot be achieved with a single-ingredient product. Our approach uses a suite of advanced, evidence-based actives that interrupt each of the five critical stages of the pigmentation factory.

settings.quote_text: As a doctor, I've seen the frustration patients experience with pigmentation that just won't budge. I developed the Aliangé pigmentation protocol to offer a clinical-grade, comprehensive solution that respects the skin's biology. We use intelligent, biomimetic ingredients that work with the skin, not against it, to gently but effectively restore its natural, even tone.

settings.name: Brightening Serum
settings.why: This powerhouse serum is the core of our pigmentation protocol, targeting all five stages of the pigmentation pathway with a potent blend of biomimetic peptides and botanical extracts.

settings.name: Biocinamide Serum
settings.why: Niacinamide is clinically proven to block the transfer of melanin to your skin cells, preventing dark spots from reaching the surface.

settings.name: Jellyfish Peptide X Serum
settings.why: Featuring Australian native Kakadu Plum, the world's richest source of Vitamin C, this serum provides powerful antioxidant protection and reduces melanin levels for a brighter complexion.

settings.name: Day Cream SPF 15
settings.why: Daily sun protection is non-negotiable. This lightweight day cream prevents new pigment from forming by shielding your skin from UV radiation—the primary trigger of hyperpigmentation.

settings.title: Doctor-Developed Protocol
settings.description: Trust in a comprehensive routine designed by a leading Australian skin expert.
The multi-acid blend begins to gently exfoliate the skin's surface, shedding the most superficial pigmented cells. Skin may start to feel smoother and appear brighter.
Niacinamide starts to interrupt the transfer of new melanin, while Oligopeptide-68 works deeper to down-regulate the master switch for pigment production.
Results become more significant. In clinical studies, 87% of users reported a more uniform tone by day 56.
Peak results are achieved. Continued use maintains the suppression of melanin overproduction, revealing your clearest, most even-toned complexion.

settings.them: Often use harsh or outdated chemicals
Only targets one part of the problem
Can lead to irritation or rebound pigmentation
settings.us: Formulated for efficacy without compromising skin health

settings.question: Is this routine suitable for melasma?
settings.answer: Yes, this multi-pathway approach is designed to be effective for various types of hyperpigmentation, including the hormonal fluctuations that can trigger melasma. By targeting multiple stages of melanin production, it provides a comprehensive strategy for managing this stubborn condition.

settings.question: How is Oligopeptide-68 different from Hydroquinone?
settings.answer: Oligopeptide-68 is a modern, biomimetic peptide that works by mimicking a natural signalling molecule in your skin to safely down-regulate pigment production. Unlike hydroquinone, which is now banned in Australia for cosmetic use due to safety concerns, Oligopeptide-68 has been shown in clinical trials to be equally or even more effective without the associated risks.

settings.question: Why do I need so many products?
settings.answer: Lasting results for pigmentation require a multi-pronged attack. Each product in the routine is formulated with specific actives that target a different stage of the pigmentation process. Using them together creates a synergistic effect that is far more powerful than any single product could be on its own.

settings.question: Is it safe to use while pregnant or breastfeeding?
settings.answer: We recommend consulting with your doctor before using any new skincare products during pregnancy or breastfeeding. Some products in this routine may be suitable, but please seek individual medical advice.

settings.headline: Ready to Leave Uneven Skin Tone Behind?
